skill.name: lark-whiteboard-cli
skill.description: Guide agents to create Lark whiteboard diagrams with whiteboard-cli, including layout rules, scene templates, rendering, and optional Lark upload steps.
skill.summary: Create structured Lark whiteboard diagrams from architecture, process, chart, and Mermaid requests.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown code fences, diagram syntax, and example identifiers. The confirmed risks are legitimate but meaningful: this community skill instructs agents to run npx/node/lark-cli commands, create local diagram files, and optionally upload or overwrite Lark whiteboards. No evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Directed External Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill explicitly instructs agents to run npm/npx, node, and lark-cli commands for dependency installation, rendering, and upload. This is expected for the skill, but it can execute downloaded packages and operate on user Lark resources, so users should review commands before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are direct and executable, and they are central to rendering and upload workflows. The risk is operational rather than hidden or malicious.

medium_findings.title: Local File Generation Through Script Templates
medium_findings.description: Several scene guides require generating JavaScript files that write diagram.json files to disk. The examples use local diagram artifacts and hardcoded filenames, but generated scripts should still be reviewed when user-supplied diagram content is inserted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-writing templates are visible and limited to diagram generation. They are not malicious, but they do create executable scripts and filesystem writes.

medium_findings.title: Cloud Whiteboard Overwrite Workflow
medium_findings.description: The upload workflow can update an existing Lark whiteboard and may overwrite existing nodes. The skill includes a dry-run and confirmation guard, which reduces risk but still makes the workflow sensitive.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The upload and overwrite commands are explicit, and the skill acknowledges destructive updates. Built-in confirmation steps make this a controlled risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence False Positives
low_findings.description: Most static Ruby backtick and weak-crypto alerts are caused by Markdown code fences, quoted DSL values, colors, SVG snippets, and diagram examples. These files are documentation, not executable Ruby or cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside Markdown examples or explanatory text. They do not execute in the skill marketplace context.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Alerts Are SVG Namespace Examples
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings in schema references point to XML namespace strings used in SVG examples and restrictions. No evidence found that these URLs are contacted for exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are standard SVG namespace declarations in documentation. The surrounding text forbids external URL references.

low_findings.title: C2 and Reconnaissance Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: Scanner hits for C2, fetch, and reconnaissance terms are diagram labels, Mermaid examples, or layout terminology. No evidence found of command-and-control behavior, system probing, or unauthorized network discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence appears in chart examples and static diagram content. The terms are not used to perform network callbacks or host inspection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Use of npx and lark-cli Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on shell commands to render diagrams and upload output. This should be permitted only with normal user command review and workspace safeguards.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The executable command pattern is directly documented. The commands match the declared purpose of the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated JavaScript Writes Diagram Files
dangerous_patterns.description: Scene templates use Node.js scripts and writeFileSync to generate diagram.json. Review generated scripts before execution when user content is inserted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script templates are clear and local. The risk depends on how generated code is composed from user requests.

content.value_statement: Complex ideas often need visual structure before teams can review them. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to design, render, and optionally upload Lark whiteboard diagrams.
actual_capabilities: Selects DSL or Mermaid rendering paths based on diagram type and user input.
Provides layout rules for architecture, flowchart, swimlane, organization, and comparison diagrams.
Includes script templates for coordinate-heavy charts such as fishbone, flywheel, funnel, and pyramid diagrams.
Defines whiteboard DSL schema rules for frames, text, shapes, connectors, SVG, and icons.
Documents rendering commands for whiteboard-cli and optional upload commands through lark-cli.
Includes pre-upload checks to avoid accidental overwrites of existing Lark whiteboards.
limitations: Requires Node.js 18 or newer and access to @larksuite/whiteboard-cli.
Lark upload workflows require lark-cli authentication and suitable workspace permissions.
Some diagram types require generated JavaScript scripts instead of direct JSON authoring.
Mermaid output has limited styling compared with the DSL path.

use_cases.title: Map a System Architecture
use_cases.description: Turn service tiers, dependencies, and databases into a structured Lark whiteboard diagram.

use_cases.title: Explain a Business Workflow
use_cases.description: Create flowcharts or swimlane diagrams that show ownership, decisions, and handoffs.

use_cases.title: Prepare Visual Reports
use_cases.description: Generate bar charts, funnels, milestones, or flywheel diagrams for planning and review meetings.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Flowchart
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Lark whiteboard flowchart for this process: [paste process]. Keep labels short and show decisions clearly.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Lark whiteboard architecture diagram for these components and dependencies: [paste details]. Use clear layers and connectors.

prompt_templates.title: Root Cause Fishbone
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a fishbone diagram for this problem: [problem]. Group causes into 4 to 6 categories and render the final image.

prompt_templates.title: Render and Upload
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate the diagram for this content, render it with whiteboard-cli, then prepare a safe Lark upload using dry-run checks first: [content and target].

output_examples.input: Create a customer onboarding swimlane for Sales, Support, and Customer Success.
output_examples.output: A rendered Lark whiteboard image with three swimlanes, ordered steps, decision points, and cross-team connectors.

output_examples.input: Visualize our checkout architecture with web, API, payment, inventory, and database components.
output_examples.output: A layered architecture diagram showing clients, services, data stores, and labeled dependency arrows.

output_examples.input: Make a Mermaid sequence diagram for login with browser, auth service, and database.
output_examples.output: A Mermaid-based whiteboard render showing participants, request flow, database lookup, and response path.

best_practices: Ask users to confirm before installing packages, running generated scripts, or uploading to Lark.
Use DSL for styled diagrams and Mermaid only for supported lightweight diagram types.
Run the documented visual review checks before delivering or uploading a rendered diagram.
anti_patterns: Do not upload to an existing whiteboard without a dry-run and explicit user confirmation.
Do not hand-write complex coordinate diagrams when the scene guide requires script generation.
Do not place connectors inside frame children, because the DSL requires root-level connectors.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates Lark whiteboard diagrams such as architecture diagrams, flowcharts, swimlanes, charts, and Mermaid-based diagrams.

faq.question: Does it require external tools?
faq.answer: Yes. Rendering requires @larksuite/whiteboard-cli, and Lark upload requires lark-cli with authentication.

faq.question: Can it upload diagrams to Lark?
faq.answer: Yes, when lark-cli is configured and the user provides a target. Existing boards require dry-run checks first.

faq.question: When should I use Mermaid?
faq.answer: Use Mermaid for mind maps, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, pie charts, or when the user provides Mermaid syntax.

faq.question: Why do some diagrams need scripts?
faq.answer: Charts such as fishbone and flywheel diagrams need calculated coordinates to avoid overlaps and poor routing.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it runs CLI commands and can update Lark whiteboards.
